preamble: The shift from the open central vowel [a] to the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] (the caret) is typically a result of vowel reduction in unstressed positions or systemic dialectal backing and raising.

examples.note: Akanje: The pre-tonic /a/ or /o/ reduces to the [ʌ] sound.
Allophonic backing of /a/ in the presence of emphatic (pharyngealized) consonants.
Various modern dialectal shifts involve the backing and raising of low vowels toward the STRUT space.

sources: Jones, D., & Ward, D. (1969). The Phonetics of Russian. Cambridge University Press.
